channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: How to Build a Task Automation Agent (That Uses APIs on Its Own)
item.description: AI is no longer just about generating text—it’s about taking action. In this tutorial, you’ll learn how to build a task automation agent that can: Decide what to do Call APIs on its own Execute...

item.title: The China Supercomputer Breach: How 10 Petabytes of Data "Walked Out" of a Tier-1 Facility
item.description: The recent news of the massive breach at the National Supercomputing Center in Tianjin (NSCC) is sending shockwaves through the tech world. While CNN and other outlets are covering the...

item.title: Git Bayesect: Bayesian Git Bisection for When Bugs Are Playing Games with You
item.description: The problem nobody talks about: what happens when git bisect can't help you because your test suite has become a liar? The Problem: When Your Test Suite Starts Gaslighting You You've been there. A...

item.title: Claude Code's Feb–Mar 2026 Updates Quietly Broke Complex Engineering — Here's the Technical Deep-Dive
item.description: A community reverse-engineered the failure modes, and the fixes are surprisingly straightforward. The Problem In February and March 2026, Anthropic shipped three simultaneous changes to Claude...

item.title: AI Coding Agents Can Verify Some of Their Work Now. Here's What They Still Miss.
item.description: Copilot and Claude Code both ship with verification features now. Copilot's Agent mode runs terminal commands, detects build failures, and iterates fixes. Claude Code plans changes across files...

item.title: It’s Simple, But Annoying: Why I Started Building Keynotif | Cahyanudien Blogs
item.description: Most notification systems ask the same thing from you. Your attention. Your reflex. Your mornings. Not because they’re malicious by design — but because they’re built around delivery, not...

item.title: Every AI Meeting Tool Wants a Monthly Fee. So I Built My Own
item.description: Somewhere along the way, productivity software stopped being something you buy and started being something you rent. You want an AI note-taker for your meetings? That's $18/month. You want it to...

item.title: AI SLOP Detector v3.1: Three Formula Refinements and the Adversarial Tester That Found Them
item.description: We shipped v2.9.0 with a scoring engine we trusted. We ran tests. Everything passed. Then we built a tool specifically designed to find cases where the score was less precise than it could be —...

item.title: NVIDIA Is Investing in an Open Source Game Engine. Here's Why That Matters.
item.description: At GDC 2026, NVIDIA did something unusual. They released a path tracing fork of Godot, the open source game engine. Not a proprietary plugin. Not a closed SDK. A full fork on GitHub under the MIT...

item.title: Why AI Detectors Produce False Positives: A Technical Analysis
item.description: An AI detector claims 95% accuracy. A student's essay gets flagged as "98% likely AI-generated." Open-and-shut case, right? Not even close. The math tells a very different story. This article...

item.title: Gemini gets notebooks to help you organize projects
item.description: Google's Gemini is getting a feature called "notebooks" to help you organize things about certain topics in a single place while using the AI chatbot, the company announced on Wednesday. You can...

item.title: Dyson made a handheld version of its iconic fans
item.description: Nearly 17 years after Dyson first announced its Air Multiplier fans - one of its first big consumer products after vacuums - the company has miniaturized their design to create a handheld personal...

item.title: Conflicting Rulings Leave Anthropic in ‘Supply-Chain Risk’ Limbo
item.description: A US appeals court ruling is at odds with a separate, lower court decision from March, leaving uncertainty about if and how the US military can use the AI company's Claude model.

item.title: Poke makes using AI agents as easy as sending a text
item.description: Poke brings AI agents to everyday users via text message by handling tasks and automations without complex setup, apps, or technical know-how.

item.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
item.description: As the US and Israel's war has ramped up, so too have hacks on US industrial sites.

item.title: OpenAI made economic proposals — here’s what DC thinks of them
item.description: Happy ceasefire day and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about Big Tech's rocky journey through the world of politics. If you're not a subscriber yet, you can do so here,...

item.title: AWS boss explains why investing billions in both Anthropic and OpenAI is an OK conflict
item.description: AWS has an ingrained culture of handling competition, he explained, because the cloud giant also competes with its partners.

item.title: I finally get the iPhone Air
item.description: I saw a lot of weird phones at Mobile World Congress last month: robot phones, cameras disguised as phones, phones for dogs. But the one that caught me most off guard was the one my friend (and...

item.title: The AI RAM shortage is also driving up SSD prices
item.description: I thought the WD Black SN850X 2TB SSD in my gaming PC was pricey when I bought it for $173 in 2024, but now that same SSD costs $649, more than what I paid for most of the parts in my PC combined....

item.title: Meta’s New AI Model Gives Mark Zuckerberg a Seat at the Big Kid’s Table
item.description: Muse Spark is Meta’s first model since its AI reboot, and the benchmarks suggest formidable performance.

item.title: Tubi is the first streamer to launch a native app within ChatGPT
item.description: Tubi becomes the first streaming service to offer an app integration within ChatGPT, the AI chatbot that millions of users turn to for answers.

item.title: The Neo Effect: How Apple’s cheapest Mac is changing the PC game
item.description: The MacBook Neo is disrupting the laptop space and putting Apple into the conversation as a value option like it’s never been before. With a price starting at $599 (or $499 for students and...

item.title: The US Army Is Building Its Own Chatbot for Combat
item.description: The AI system, trained on real military data, is meant to give soldiers mission-critical information.

item.title: New Chaos Variant Targets Misconfigured Cloud Deployments, Adds SOCKS Proxy
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new variant ofmalware called Chaosthat'scapable of hitting misconfigured cloud deployments, marking an expansion of the botnet's targeting infrastructure....

item.title: 5 Burning Questions About Elon Musk’s Terafab Chip Partnership with Intel
item.description: Intel’s role in Elon Musk’s ambitious chip venture is still murky, raising questions about what the partnership actually entails—and whether it can work at all.

item.title: Anthropic’s New Product Aims to Handle the Hard Part of Building AI Agents
item.description: Amid rapid enterprise growth, Anthropic is trying to lower the barrier to entry for businesses to build AI agents with Claude.

item.title: Microsoft’s executive shake-up continues as developer division chief resigns
item.description: Microsoft is losing another veteran executive. Julia Liuson, head of Microsoft's developer division (DevDiv), is resigning from the software giant after 34 years. Liuson spent the past 12 years...

item.title: Masjesu Botnet Emerges as DDoS-for-Hire Service Targeting Global IoT Devices
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have lifted the curtain on a stealthy botnet that's designed for distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks. Called Masjesu, the botnet has been advertised via Telegram...

item.title: Meta is reentering the AI race with a new model called Muse Spark
item.description: Meta Superintelligence Labs is launching its first model since Mark Zuckerberg spent billions overhauling the company's AI efforts. Called Muse Spark, the model now powers the Meta AI app and the...

item.title: Astropad’s Workbench reimagines remote desktop for AI agents, not IT support
item.description: Astropad’s Workbench lets users remotely monitor and control AI agents on Mac Minis from iPhone or iPad, with low-latency streaming and mobile access.
